title: Rotate cross-source spot check on committed video fixture

metricComparisons.description: raising rotate should materially reorganize framing/detail on decoded media
positive rotation should materially bias the rendered decoded-media stereo field toward the right channel

expectedVideo: the decoded fixture should rotate cleanly without black wedges or undefined edges
the stronger rotation should produce a visible framing change on the committed clip
expectedAudio: fixture audio should follow the same stereo-rotation law as the osc case
the rendered mix should stay stable without codec-like crackle or level collapse
manualChecks: Does rotate remain musically and visually intentional on decoded media?
Does the stronger setting still feel like one coherent AV operator?
